manufacturer.comment: Manufactured under license; broadly distributed by Seeed Studio.

status.comment: DigiKey shows in stock with 29 units at $237.50 as of 2026-04-26; manufacturer lead time 12 weeks.

architecture-family.comment: Cortex-A72 application cores are ARMv8 (aarch64); on-die Cortex-R5F MCUs are ARMv7-R but not the application ISA.

open-source-hardware.comment: OSHWA-certified (US002120); design files published in BeagleBoard.org's GitLab/OpenBeagle.

country-of-origin.comment: BeagleBoard.org Foundation is headquartered in Michigan, USA; PCBs assembled in China.

soc.comment: Member of TI's K3 J721E family.

soc-vendor.comment: TI (Texas Instruments) — `ti` tag not yet defined in attributes.json; using `other` per ongoing schema gap (same workaround as beaglebone-black). User has flagged for batch schema fix.

cpu-cores.comment: 2× Cortex-A72 application cores. Additional 6× Cortex-R5F real-time MCUs and 2× C66x + 1× C7x DSPs not counted as application CPU cores.

cpu-architecture.value: 2× Cortex-A72 (+ 6× Cortex-R5F MCUs, 2× C66x DSP, 1× C7x DSP)

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A72 application cores at up to 2.0 GHz.

process-node.comment: TDA4VM is a TI 16 nm FinFET part per TI processor SDK documentation; not directly stated on the BeagleBoard product page.

opengl-es-version.comment: PowerVR Series8XE supports OpenGL ES 3.2; driver coverage on Linux is via TI's vendor stack.

vulkan-support.comment: No Vulkan driver shipped in TI's vendor stack or in mainline; PowerVR open-source driver work has not landed Vulkan for this generation.

npu.comment: C7x vector DSP + Matrix Multiply Accelerator (MMA) form the deep-learning accelerator.

npu-performance-tops.comment: 8 TOPS @ INT8 from C7x+MMA at 1.0 GHz (vendor figure).

hw-video-decode.comment: TDA4VM datasheet lists H.264 and H.265 (HEVC) decode up to 4K@60. No VP9/AV1.

hw-video-encode.comment: TDA4VM datasheet lists H.264 encode up to 4K@60. No H.265 encode on this SoC variant.

ram-min-gb.comment: Single 4 GB SKU; no other RAM options sold.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 E-key socket is for Wi-Fi/cellular modules (PCIe/USB/SDIO), not M-key NVMe storage.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; M.2 E-key slot accepts a user-supplied Wi-Fi/Bluetooth module.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; available only via M.2 E-key add-on.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB-C carrying USB 3.0 data and power.

poe-support.comment: No PoE support; no PoE HAT/cape ecosystem for the BeagleBone P8/P9 layout.

hdmi-outputs.comment: Mini DisplayPort replaces the micro-HDMI of the earlier BeagleBone AI; no HDMI port on the AI-64.

max-display-resolution.comment: TDA4VM display subsystem supports 4K@60 over DisplayPort.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm analog audio jack on board; audio routed via DisplayPort or via cape/USB add-ons.

gpio-pins.comment: P8 (46 pins) + P9 (50 pins) BeagleBone cape headers. Reports vary on P8 width; some sources cite 46+46. Total expansion pin count taken from BeagleBoard expansion docs.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Uses BeagleBone P8/P9 cape headers, not the 40-pin Raspberry Pi GPIO layout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Compatible with BeagleBone capes, not Raspberry Pi HATs.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: 1× M.2 E-key (PCIe + USB + SDIO, primarily for Wi-Fi)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: M.2 2230 E-key slot; intended for wireless modules but also exposes a PCIe lane.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, UART, CAN, PWM all available on P8/P9 cape headers.

power-input.comment: Vendor recommends a 5V ≥ 3A (15 W) supply via USB-C or barrel jack.

idle-power-w.comment: Approximate measurement (~5 W idle) by a community user using a UPS module; treat as ballpark, not a Phoronix-grade benchmark.

load-power-w.comment: No reliable independent measurement of full-load draw found. Vendor PSU recommendation is 15 W headroom; this is a TDP allowance, not a measured load figure.

cooling-required.comment: Board ships with a small heatsink on the TDA4VM; sustained AI/vision workloads benefit from active airflow but no fan is mandated by the vendor.

rtc-battery-header.comment: No clear documentation of an onboard RTC coin-cell holder or battery header was found in the vendor docs surfaced for this pass; community add-ons exist but on-board provision is unconfirmed.

form-factor-class.comment: BeagleBone-cape form factor; larger than the classic BeagleBone Black footprint and not aligned with Pi-standard or any ITX size.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: BeagleBone P8/P9 cape mounting pattern, not RPi standard.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Cortex-A72 cores, basic peripherals, U-Boot, and Ethernet are upstream (TI K3 platform). Vendor-only stack still required for full GPU, C7x/MMA NPU (TIDL), DSP, and vision accelerators. Armbian/BeagleBoard official images all use TI vendor 6.12/6.18 kernels.

official-os-image.comment: BeagleBoard.org publishes maintained Debian 13 images (XFCE and minimal) with multiple kernel options.

distros-with-images.comment: Debian (official BeagleBoard images), Ubuntu and Debian Forky via Armbian; Kali, Home Assistant, OMV, openHAB images also published via Armbian.

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot has board support for the J721E BeagleBone AI-64; TI ROM bootloader handles initial stages.

long-term-support.comment: BeagleBoard.org has historically maintained images for many years (e.g., BeagleBone Black is still supported), but no specific committed LTS window is published for the AI-64.

android-support.comment: No official or community-maintained Android image surfaced; only Debian/Ubuntu/Armbian variants offered.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Initial 2022 launch MSRP. As of 2026-04-26, DigiKey street price is $237.50 — single-config product, so base = top.

available-regions.comment: Distributed via DigiKey, Mouser, RS Components, Element14/Newark, OKdo, Vilros — global retail footprint.

typical-lead-time.comment: DigiKey shows 29 units in stock as of 2026-04-26; manufacturer lead time is 12 weeks for restocking.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive multi-chapter docs site, schematics, board files, getting-started, expansion docs; one notch below the very best (RPi/Hardkernel) due to occasional gaps and SDK fragmentation.

community-size.comment: BeagleBoard ecosystem broadly is large/established; AI-64 specifically attracts a smaller subset focused on TI K3/AI work.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run Discourse forum (forum.beagleboard.org) with active threads.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Inherits the BeagleBone cape ecosystem (P8/P9 compatible), but many older capes don't fit AI-64's larger footprint without adjustments. Camera and display ecosystem is decent but not as broad as RPi accessories.
